page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfg6.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. In CPU-focused tests, it achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1436.68 and a multi-core score of 30666.35, while the PassMark CPU score is recorded at 29517.51. The memory subsystem delivers a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1705.41 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 48.22 GB/sec. For database operations, the instance processes 28.91 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 11442.59 on PassMark database tests. It also handles light LLM inference, processing 45.98 prompt tokens per second and 12.4 text generation tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

The ecs.hfg6.8xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without the need for local NVMe storage. The absence of bundled local storage means storage-heavy applications must rely on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O latency. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated compute resources, this instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate text and image processing tasks.
